stories.title: Germany says it won’t drop internal border checks
stories.summary: LUXEMBOURG — German Interior Minister Alexander Dobrindt pushed back Thursday against a European Commission request that Germany drop its internal border checks. The Commission said the checks would no longer be needed because of sweeping changes to the bloc’s migration policies. The EU has agreed on measures to boost control over who crosses into the bloc, including by strengthening screening procedures and providing more support for the countries that receive the most migrants. Thes

stories.title: Paris and Berlin pitch ‘gradual integration’ for EU hopefuls amid frustration in Western Balkans
stories.summary: EU candidate countries should be able to gradually unlock privileges such as partial access to the bloc’s single market to keep them motivated while they wait for full membership, Paris and Berlin have suggested in a new joint paper. In the discussion paper, circulated ahead of a Western Balkans summit and seen by POLITICO, aspiring EU members could also win observer status at key meetings, as well as other perks, to avoid disenchantment with the process. The idea would be to bring “

stories.title: Europe’s far right exploit Henry Nowak murder in UK with populist rhetoric on race
stories.summary: Polish, Spanish and French populists focus on clips of teenager’s dying moments and accuse UK of descending ‘into depths of the earth’ UK politics live – latest updates Polish far-right politicians have claimed that the murder of Henry Nowak symbolises “Britain’s descent into the depths of the earth” as populists from France, Spain and Japan focused on harrowing clips of his dying moments. Despite pleas from Nowak’s family for people not to exploit the killing for political gain and to focus on

stories.title: Jesse Calhoun pleads not guilty to fifth murder charge in Oregon court
stories.summary: Prosecutors say they intend to try all five killings together next year after brief arraignment in Portland Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email An accused serial killer in Oregon was arraigned on Wednesday for the murder of a fifth woman. A lawyer for Jesse Calhoun, who was already facing charges in the deaths of four women whose bodies were discovered in 2022 and 2023, entered a not guilty plea for the second-degree murder of Ashley Real, a 22-year-old who previously alleged he ha

stories.title: Ukraine moves closer to EU accession after Hungary deal
stories.summary: Budapest will likely drop its veto on Kyiv joining the EU after reaching a deal on rights for Ukraine's ethnic Hungarian minority. But it remains opposed to Ukraine's accession being fast-tracked.

stories.title: Charges dropped against Budapest mayor over 2025 Pride march
stories.summary: Budapest's liberal Mayor Gergely Karacsony had been charged with helping to organise the banned 2025 march.

stories.title: Magyar announces agreement with Ukraine on rights of Hungarian minority
stories.summary: According to the Hungarian Prime Minister, Ukraine’s commitments will be reflected in the action plan agreed upon with the European Union.

stories.title: Trump administration proposes 25% tariffs on Brazil despite US trade surplus
stories.summary: US claims world’s 10th-biggest economy engages in ‘unreasonable’ trade practices that ‘restrict US commerce’ Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Trump administration proposed 25% tariffs on imports from Brazil , charging that the world’s 10th-biggest economy engages in trade practices that are “unreasonable’’ and that “burden or restrict US commerce”. Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said he received the decision “with indignation”. The Brazil president also blamed the decision by the

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Örömmel fogadnék egy meghívást Washingtonba
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök a Spiegelnek azt is mondta, hogy szerinte próbáltak udvariasak lenni Sulyok Tamással.

stories.title: Magyarország is támogatta, így az EU megkezdi a csatlakozási tárgyalásokat Ukrajnával és Moldovával
stories.summary: Ez az első tárgyalási klaszter hivatalos megnyitásának előkészítését jelenti.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a parlamentben akarja végleg megroppantani a Fideszt
stories.summary: Magyar Péter miniszterelnökként a kampányban látott kemény stílusban osztja ki a fideszes politikusokat a parlamentben. A beszólogatásokkal már most elejét akarja venni, hogy a választási vereségtől megrendült Fidesz később támadni tudja a kormányát, amikor előjönnek a kényesebb ügyek.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Egy olyan országot kell építenünk, ahol nemcsak mi szeretjük a hazánkat, hanem a hazánk is viszontszeret minket
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök a trianoni szerződés nemzeti emléknapján mondott beszédet a Kossuth téren.

stories.title: Új román törvény feszegeti Nyirő József és Wass Albert helyét az erdélyi emlékezetben
stories.summary: Az RMDSZ is támogatta a törvényt, amely alapján ismét előkerült Wass és Nyirő neve. Wassnak már eddig is legfeljebb csellel lehetett szobrot állítani Romániában, Nyirő nevét viszont utca és több iskola is viseli Erdélyben.

stories.title: Saját részvényeit veszi a tőzsdén a Magyar Telekom, így segíti a tulajdonosait
stories.summary: Ha egy cég a készpénzéből a tulajdonosainak szeretne juttatni, fizethet osztalékot (dividend) vagy belekezdhet egy részvényvisszavásárlási (buyback) programba.

stories.title: Aranyat ér az új ügyfél a bankoknak, akár 150 ezer forint is jár nekünk
stories.summary: Egyre feljebb srófolják a bankok azt az összeget, amennyit bezsebelhet tőlük egy új ügyfél a számlanyitásért. Az elmúlt hetekben újabb jóváírási akciók indultak, és a már meglévőket is vonzóbbá tették – derül ki a BiztosDöntés.hu által összegyűjtött adatokból. Az esetenként százezer forint feletti ajándékpénzekért cserébe viszont teljes elköteleződést várnak el a pénzintézetek.

stories.title: A magyar könyv ünnepe: négynapos fesztivállá alakul Budapest júniusban (x)
stories.summary: Június 11. és 14. között ismét a könyveké lesz a főszerep Budapest belvárosában: a Magyar Könyvkiadók és Könyvterjesztők Egyesülése megrendezi a 97. Ünnepi Könyvhetet. A Vörösmarty tér, a Vigadó tér és a Duna-korzó négy napon át a kortárs magyar irodalom, a könyvkiadás és a kulturális találkozások központjává válik, ahol mintegy kétszáz kiállító, több száz program, könyvbemutató, közel ezer dedikálás, koncert és beszélgetés várja az érdeklődőket.

stories.title: Meghalt Marjane Satrapi, a Persepolis rendezője
stories.summary: Az iráni–francia képregényes-filmrendező a saját gyerekkoráról szóló Persepolis rajzolójaként lett világhírű, 56 éves volt.

stories.title: Szijjártó alatt már meg sem lepődött senki, hogy nincs, Orbán Anita most 15 év után visszahozza
stories.summary: A legtöbb országban a külügyminisztériumok céljait és prioritásait részletesen kidolgozott stratégiákban szokás összefoglalni. 2011-ben még Orbán alatt is készült nyilvánosan elérhető külpolitikai stratégia, mai szemmel meglepő alapvetésekkel. Szijjártó alatt hűlt helye volt, de a Magyar-kormány újból lefekteti, mik alapján képzeli el a nemzetközi kapcsolatokat és az ország helyét a világban.

stories.title: Optimizmus vagy végkiárusítás: miért vesznek fel ilyen eszméletlen sok hitelt a magyarok?
stories.summary: Megduplázódtak a lakáshitelek, már négy hónap alatt ezer milliárd forintot vettek fel belőlük – derül ki a Magyar Nemzeti Bank (MNB) friss adataiból.

stories.title: Először igazolták az afrikai sertéspestist Magyarországon házi sertésekben
stories.summary: Az országos főállatorvos azonnali hatósági intézkedéseket rendelt el.

stories.title: Megbuherálta a rendszerét, évekig fékezte a propaganda megjelenését a Hírkereső tulajdonosa
stories.summary: Holló Gábor azt állítja: több közvélemény-kutatást is finanszírozott a Tisza Pártnak, és összesen nagyjából 50 millió forinttal támogatta Magyarékat.

stories.title: Pimasznak és illegálisnak tartja az ukrán jegybank a pénzszállítók elleni akciót, amiről Orbán Viktor döntött
stories.summary: Az ukrán jegybank a Telex tényfeltáró cikkére reagált. Azért is bíznak a szigorú fellépésben, mert meg vannak győződve arról, hogy a jogállamiságnak az EU alapvető értékének kell maradnia.

stories.title: Az évtizedes és sokmilliárdos parkfenntartási bizniszhez vezettek az Őrsi Gergelyt és fideszes politikusokat érintő korrupciós ügy szálai
stories.summary: Már 2009-ben GVH-vizsgálat indult a budapesti parkfenntartási piac szereplőivel szemben, de az ágazat működése se a 2010-es, se a 2019-es politikai fordulat után nem változott meg. Az ügyészség szerint az egyik parkfenntartó cég több milliárd forint kenőpénzt osztott vissza fideszes, MSZP-s és momentumos politikusoknak.

stories.title: Magyar Péter azt kívánja, legyen sokkal kényelmesebb az írek uniós elnöksége, mint a magyarok elnöksége volt
stories.summary: Magyar Péter az ír miniszterelnökkel tárgyalt Budapesten.

stories.title: „Győzött a szabadság” – civil szervezetek reagáltak arra, hogy ejtették a vádat a pécsi és a budapesti Pride szervezői ellen
stories.summary: A közleményt kiadó civil szervezetek azt írták, 2025-től kezdve közösen dolgoztak azért, hogy visszaállítsák a gyülekezési jog szabad gyakorlását.

stories.title: Emlékszik még a januári rezsistopra? Az MVM biztos szívesen elfelejtené már
stories.summary: Rég vége már a télnek, de a közműcég még a nyár elején is az összevissza érkező számlák miatt kénytelen magyarázkodni a semmiből a nyakába zúdított kampányajándék jóvoltából.

stories.title: Hungary’s new foreign policy strategy is taking shape, and it couldn’t differ more from what we’ve seen in the past fifteen years
stories.summary: In most countries, the foreign policy goals and priorities are typically summarized in detailed, publicly available documents. Under Orbán, 2011 was the last time such a strategy was drafted for Hungary. Szijjártó didn't bother with it, but now, the new Hungarian government is establishing a new vision for the country's place in the world.

stories.title: Sulyok szerint sürgős eljárás keretében vizsgálja a Velencei Bizottság, hogy a Tisza állami tisztségviselőket akar lemondatni
stories.summary: A köztársasági elnök Facebook-posztjából az is kiderült: az Európa Tanács alkotmányjogi tanácsadó testületének munkatársai készek Magyarországra utazni, hogy felmérjék a helyzetet.

stories.title: A Fidesz a padlón, de Magyar Péter még ott is üti
stories.summary: Ha csak a Facebookot nézzük, a Fidesz most rosszabbul áll, mint az óellenzék a 2022-es kiütéses választási vereség után. Orbán a közösségi médiában is teljes sorcserét vezényel le, miközben Vitézy Dávid körül vonatkultusz épül, Hadházynak meg visszavonultatja a mezszámát az NBA. Lájkbajnokság.

stories.title: Brazen and illegal – National Bank of Ukraine on raid against bank vehicles ordered by Orbán
stories.summary: The National Bank of Ukraine reacted to Telex’s investigative article and said they were confident there would be a firm response from the authorities, as the rule of law must remain a fundamental value of the EU.

stories.title: Az ukrán külügyminiszter és az Európai Bizottság is üdvözli a Magyarország és Ukrajna között zajló tárgyalásokat
stories.summary: „Új fejezetet nyitunk az ukrán–magyar kapcsolatokban – egy olyan fejezetet, amely a kölcsönös tiszteleten, a bizalmon és közös európai jövőnkön alapul” – írta Andrij Szibiha.

stories.title: The EU should fast-track Ukraine’s membership of the club – it has the most to gain | Mujtaba Rahman
stories.summary: Many EU governments oppose it, but the strategic advantages of swift admission could be immense Russia’s war on Ukraine is now in its fifth year and a ceasefire remains elusive. The US’s attention is divided, limiting external pressure for compromise, while Moscow and Kyiv both still believe they can strengthen their respective negotiating positions through battlefield gains. At some point, however, a deal will have to be done. The parameters of that deal are already understood by negotiators on

stories.title: What the Hellenic! Why is Christopher Nolan’s new Greek epic entirely devoid of Greeks?
stories.summary: Set to be this year’s biggest blockbuster, The Odyssey’s cast has been selected to ‘represent the world’. Fair enough – except that one key country seems to have gone completely unrepresented … There are the American accents, gleaming body suits and a muddy Dunkirk palette. And then there is Lupita Nyong’o as Helen of Troy, a casting choice that recently drew racist attacks from the usual moaners of the internet , including Elon Musk, who complained it wasn’t authentic. stories.title: Canada calls for US and Mexico free trade deal to be renewed for 16 years
stories.summary: “The Agreement is highly beneficial to each of our countries and to the integrated North American economy," a letter conveying Canada’s recommendation, sent to the US and Mexico trade representatives, said.

stories.title: Experts criticise plan for American-only Ebola quarantine centre in Kenya
stories.summary: Plan departs from policy of bringing CDC staff back to US for treatment and offering support to all health workers Former top US officials and other experts are urging the Trump administration to abandon plans for an Ebola quarantine and treatment centre in Kenya, as the union for workers with the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) calls for Americans exposed to Ebola to be brought home for treatment. Soon after the US revealed it was setting up a field hospital in Kenya for the

stories.title: UK’s Starmer: Musk is trying to ‘whip up division’ over murder case
stories.summary: LONDON — Keir Starmer accused Elon Musk of interfering in British politics Thursday after the owner of the X social media platform posted a flurry of comments about the murder of a British teenager. Musk, a long-standing critic of the U.K.’s prime minister, has published more than 100 posts, replies and retweets about British politics since May 27. Many of those have focused on the murder of Henry Nowak — an 18-year-old man killed in the southern England city of Southampton last Decem

stories.title: New forced labor tariffs are in line with US-EU deal, insists Trump trade official
stories.summary: PARIS — Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said Thursday that proposed new U.S. tariffs on European exports are compatible with the transatlantic trade deal struck last summer at President Donald Trump’s Turnberry golf resort in Scotland. “The Turnberry agreement says that the European Union agrees and acknowledges that the United States can impose tariffs up to a certain level,” Greer said in Paris on the sidelines of a ministerial meeting at the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Deve

stories.title: Protesters clash with police in Albania over Jared Kushner-backed luxury resort – video
stories.summary: Protests in Albania over a proposed luxury resort backed by Jared Kushner , Donald Trump’s son-in-law, are expected to intensify after opponents rejected an offer from the country’s prime minister 'to discuss solutions'. stories.title: Civilians flee Mogadishu as Somali troops and opposition-allied militias trade fire
stories.summary: Violence flares before protests on Thursday over president’s decision to remain in office after his term expired Fierce clashes have taken place between government troops and militias allied with the opposition in Somalia’s capital, Mogadishu, damaging property and forcing some civilians to flee. In the runup to the fighting, which started on Wednesday afternoon, opposition leaders embedded with militias set up positions in their clan strongholds the city. Continue reading...

stories.title: Watch: New footage shows moment Iranian drone struck Kuwait airport
stories.summary: One person was killed and more than 60 injured in the Iranian drone strike on Wednesday.

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Russia losing on the ground so pivots to air war, say analysts
stories.summary: Minimal gains on battlefield as Kyiv largely halts Moscow’s spring-summer offensive; Ukraine missile maker tests homegrown Patriot alternative. What we know on day 1,562 Russia’s failure to advance on the battlefield is why it is escalating its air raids on major Ukrainian cities , analysts say. The Institute for the Study of War (ISW) thinktank said the strikes were also aimed at distracting from the impact of Ukrainian long-range attacks into Russia . stories.title: Greenland is part of Denmark … ‘for now,’ Rubio says
stories.summary: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio acknowledged Wednesday that Greenland is part of Denmark “for now,” while signaling that Washington remains deeply engaged in discussions over the Arctic island’s future role in Western security. Testifying before the House Foreign Affairs Committee, Rubio sidestepped a question about whether Washington needed to own territory within NATO to defend it, instead pointing to ongoing talks with Denmark and Greenland over the island’s role in collective

stories.title: Turbulent skies: The stealth erosion of EC261
stories.summary: For more than two decades, EU Regulation EC261 has been one of Europe’s clearest consumer success stories. It transformed air passenger rights from vague promises into enforceable protections, while creating powerful incentives for airlines to improve operational reliability. The results are measurable: EU passengers today are 70 percent less likely to face delays exceeding three hours and face 20 percent less same-day cancellations than travellers in the United States, where no comparable syste

stories.title: Von der Leyen’s AI pick triggers conflict-of-interest criticism
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The appointment of Siemens’ chairman as a European Commission adviser on industrial AI is triggering a backlash in Brussels, weeks after the German engineering giant helped secure a rollback of the EU’s AI rules. “My first reaction was just: Wow,” said Kim van Sparrentak, a Dutch lawmaker who led the work on the AI file for the Greens in the European Parliament.  “They fought hard against AI rules for themselves, they lobby against technological sovereignty, and

stories.title: Netanayahu’s been ‘a great partner’, says Trump – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. See our full report on the latest Middle East news The Kuwaiti defence ministry said it intercepted 13 ballistic missiles and 17 drones launched by Iran today. A drone and missile attack on Kuwait’s international airport killed one person, which Kuwaiti authorities identified as an Indian national. It is the first reported death in a Gulf state since the US and Iran agreed to a ceasefire in April. Continue reading...

As we know, the government has set up the bill as a wedge for the opposition, by including both tax changes to CGT and negative gearing and tax cuts for workers. It opens the possibility of the Greens and Coalition teaming up in parliament to support extending

stories.title: UK to challenge EU over ‘devastating’ plans to almost halve tariff-free steel import quotas
stories.summary: Business secretary to meet European counterpart on Friday as EU industry leaders worry about retaliatory measures by UK Business live – latest updates The UK business secretary, Peter Kyle, is to raise concerns about EU plans to dramatically reduce tariff-free imports of British steel with its trade commissioner, Maroš Šefčovič, in Brussels on Friday. The UK steel industry has previously warned of “devastating” consequences from the new quota system being planned by the EU, which will cut overal

stories.title: Financier Lex Greensill banned from running UK companies for nine years
stories.summary: Founder of Greensill Capital says there was no finding he acted dishonestly after his company collapsed owing £1.6bn The disgraced former financier Lex Greensill has been banned from running a UK company for nine years after he was judged to be unfit because of the 2021 collapse of his £1.6bn supply chain invoicing firm. The government’s Insolvency Service said on Thursday that Greensill had signed a disqualification undertaking, bringing the case to an end before a trial was due to begin on 8 J

stories.title: ‘Embarrassing’: pressure on Merz after Germany’s failure to win UN security council seat
stories.summary: Criticism comes from across political spectrum after blow to Friedrich Merz’s government Germany’s unprecedented failure to win one of the rotating seats on the UN security council has prompted an intense round of soul searching in Berlin, and raised questions about its claims to international leadership under Friedrich Merz. The council vote on Wednesday, which elected Austria and Portugal to a two-year term along with Trinidad and Tobago and Zimbabwe, was a blow to Merz’s struggling government

stories.title: Dead but deportable: US immigration judge signed order to eject teen murder victim
stories.summary: North Carolina judge said Levi Mendez-Maldonado failed to show up in court – even after being told he had died in 2024 An immigration judge in Charlotte, North Carolina , recently ordered the deportation of a young man who was killed in 2024, citing his failure to appear in court. Judge Amy Lee ordered the removal of Levi Mendez-Maldonado in absentia on 21 May. Mendez-Maldonado, originally from Honduras, came to the United States as an unaccompanied minor at age 17 and was murdered in a shooting

stories.title: EU concerns grow over Albania's Trump-linked megaproject
stories.summary: A huge luxury resort on Albania's protected coast linked to Jared Kushner, son-in-law of US President Donald Trump, is sparking protests, environmental concerns and scrutiny as the country advances toward EU membership.

stories.title: US House approves war powers resolution to rein in Trump's military actions
stories.summary: House lawmakers voted 215-208 on the resolution, with four Republicans joining Democrats, an outcome that saw cheers erupting in the House chamber in what is a clear rebuke of Trump’s war strategy.

stories.title: Albania: Fourth day of unrest as Trump-linked resort sparks protests
stories.summary: On the fourth consecutive day of protests, demonstrators clashed with police in central Tirana on 3 June over a luxury resort project linked to the Trump family.

stories.title: Video shows moment drone hits Kuwait airport terminal
stories.summary: Surveillance footage captured the moment a drone struck a passenger terminal at Kuwait International Airport, killing one person and injuring dozens in the latest escalation involving Iran.

stories.title: Greenland is part of Denmark 'for now,' US Secretary of State Marco Rubio says
stories.summary: The US is reported to have had 17 military facilities and more than 10,000 troops on Greenland at the height of the Cold War.

stories.title: Heavy gunfire in Somali capital as row over election delay escalates
stories.summary: The opposition has called for protests after the president's term in office was extended by a year.

stories.title: Clash at protest against Kushner linked Albania resort project
stories.summary: Video shows security personnel arguing with demonstrators before pushing one protester to the ground and dragging him away. The incident took place during a rally organised by environmental groups opposing a luxury resort project linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of US President Donald Trump.

stories.title: One killed and dozens injured in Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait airport
stories.summary: Iran says the attack on Kuwait was in retaliation for earlier US strikes on an Iranian oil tanker and island.

stories.title: Oman resists US pressure to break ties with Iran over strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Sultanate says talks with Tehran are limited to lawful management of waterway, but Washington has doubts about neutrality Oman is resisting US pressure to break its links with Iran, and insists it has only been negotiating with Tehran on a future management system for the strait of Hormuz that would be compliant with international law. stories.title: Rebel attacks in eastern DRC kill 30 people and hamper Ebola response
stories.summary: Islamic State-linked militia blamed for raids in North Kivu as governor says three patients with disease fled clinics Rebel attacks around a town that is one of the centres of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo have left more than 30 people dead over the past few days, complicating the response to the disease. At least 10 people were massacred in raids on three villages around the city of Beni, in North Kivu, in the early hours of Wednesday morning. Continue reading...

stories.title: Bondi attack hero Ahmed al-Ahmed charged with assaulting his father
stories.summary: Police allege the 44-year-old lauded for disarming one of the gunmen in December’s attack put his father in a headlock Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Sydney man who disarmed one of the Bondi terror attackers has been charged with assaulting his father. Ahmed al-Ahmed, 44, is accused of allegedly putting his father in a headlock on 9 March at a house in Bankstown, in the city’s west. Continue reading...

stories.title: Bob Harris steps down from BBC Radio 2 shows because of ill health
stories.summary: Veteran broadcaster makes ‘one of hardest decisions of my entire life’ weeks after revealing spread of cancer to spine The veteran broadcaster Bob Harris has announced he is stepping down from BBC Radio 2 after 30 years so he can focus on “getting well again”, six weeks after revealing his prostate cancer had spread into his upper spine. The 80-year-old host of The Country Show and Sounds of the 70s, known as “Whispering” Bob Harris, said his health problems were forcing him to step down and tha

stories.title: Alarm as once-eradicated flesh-eating parasite found in calf in Texas
stories.summary: USDA confirms first case of New World screwworm in cattle in six decades, posing threat to livestock industry A flesh-eating parasite rarely seen in the US in six decades has been found in a calf in Texas, agriculture officials said, in an alarming development for the country’s cattle industry. The New World screwworm fly ( NWS ) was confirmed in the animal in the south of the state, about 50 miles from the Mexico border, Brooke Rollins, the agriculture secretary, said late on Wednesday. Continu

stories.title: Starmer accuses Musk of trying to ‘whip up division’ in UK over Henry Nowak murder
stories.summary: PM says Britons are ‘reasonable, tolerant people’ and backs MP’s legal action against Grok firm over fake sexualised images UK politics live – latest updates Elon Musk is “interfering in our politics” and attempting to create division, Keir Starmer has said in a significant toughening of government language about the X owner. It comes after weeks of posts by Musk on his social media platform about the murder of Henry Nowak , many of which have used far-right themes and talking points. Continue r

stories.title: Eight alleged US thieves indicted for stealing $5m worth of cheese, beef and cigarettes
stories.summary: ‘Retail theft ring’ stole goods from logistics sites in Pennsylvania, Virginia and New Jersey and sold them in New York, officials say Eight people were indicted this week in New York in connection with what prosecutors describe as a “wide-ranging retail theft ring” that stole nearly $5m worth of goods – from steaks to cheeses to copper wiring and cigarettes. The Manhattan district attorney’s office announced charges against the eight individuals on Wednesday, accusing them of “conspiring to imp

stories.title: SpaceX targets biggest ever stock market debut, putting Musk on course to be trillionaire
stories.summary: IPO could raise up to $75bn, giving SpaceX market value of $1.77tn as it sets up Musk for extraordinary wealth Elon Musk’s SpaceX is looking to raise $75bn (£55bn) from its blockbuster stock market listing next week as the rocket company aims for the largest initial public offering ever. If the stock market launch – primed for 12 June – goes as planned, founder Musk, the world’s wealthiest person, could make history as the first trillionaire. Continue reading...

stories.title: Starmer to meet Henry Nowak’s family this afternoon, No 10 says – UK politics live
stories.summary: The private meeting will take place in Downing Street Kemi Badenoch has posted a message on social media saying she met Henry Nowak’s father, mother and stepmother this morning. She praises their courage, and says: Henry’s family do not want anger to tear communities apart. They are a family who have friends across faith and race, and so did Henry. His family want his memory to help bring our society together. Everyone knows I have strong views about how we should deal with equality under the la

stories.title: Hezbollah rejects Israel-Lebanon truce as Trump scrambles to end Iran war
stories.summary: Group calls ceasefire a ‘roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people’, throwing regional peace talks into doubt Middle East crisis – live updates Hezbollah has rejected a US-brokered ceasefire plan agreed by the Lebanese and Israeli governments, throwing the future of a truce in Lebanon and regional peace negotiations into question. The group’s leader, Naim Qassem, called the ceasefire plan a “roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people” in a statement delivered on Thursday. Continue

stories.title: Moment explosion erupts at Russian oil terminal in St. Petersburg after Ukrainian drone attack
stories.summary: Black smoke billowed over St. Petersburg after a Ukrainian drone strike sparked a fire at an oil terminal, according to Ukrainian and Russian officials. The attack came days before President Vladimir Putin is due to attend a major economic forum in the city.

stories.title: Germany sees record number of discrimination claims in 2025
stories.summary: The federal commissioner for anti-discrimination has said the government's reform plans, the first in 20 years, are inadequate and won't provide the resources and funding needed to combat discrimination.

stories.title: Tanzania's president visits Russia amid frayed Western ties
stories.summary: President Samia Suluhu Hassan is on a three-day visit to Russia, traveling with a business delegation. Relations with Western nations have cratered in recent months amid criticism of Tanzania's handling of dissent.

stories.title: Erin Brockovich: Environmentalist against the data center boom
stories.summary: Do you know where a data center is due to be constructed in your area? An increasing number of US citizens can answer "yes" — thanks to a map created by Erin Brockovich. DW explains why people need to know.

stories.title: India news: Delhi orders fire safety crackdown after deadly hotel fire
stories.summary: Hours after a fire at a New Delhi hotel killed 21 people, the establishment's owner has been arrested. Delhi is also launching a fire safety inspection.

stories.title: Iran war: Even a peace deal won't fix energy crunch
stories.summary: The growing energy and supply chain crisis is unlikely to ease, even if the US and Iran step back from the brink. Experts warn that reopening the Strait of Hormuz and repairing infrastructure could take months or years.

stories.title: China bans 4 New Zealand lawmakers after their Taiwan trip
stories.summary: Beijing says it is barring a group of New Zealand lawmakers from entering China for having visited Taiwan in May. It is the first time such a ban has been imposed on politicians from New Zealand.

stories.title: 2026 World Cup: 6 things you need to know
stories.summary: The 2026 World Cup is to be bigger than all of its predecessors. Whether it will be better is an entirely different question. DW fills you in on what you need to know.

stories.title: $850 million Obama Presidential Center prepares for opening
stories.summary: The museum dedicated to former US President Barack Obama is set to open on June 19. Spanning 19.3 acres, the campus will house a museum, library, and park.

stories.title: 100 days of Iran war: Gulf states grapple with security and economic consequences
stories.summary: The US-Israel war with Iran has forced the Gulf states to reassess long-held assumptions about security, alliances and stability in their region. What does this mean for the future?

stories.title: Germany news: Number of millionaires is growing, study shows
stories.summary: Around 1.78 million people in Germany were classified as high-net-worth individuals or people who have at least $1 million in liquid assets. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Marjane Satrapi, author of 'Persepolis,' dies at 56
stories.summary: The Iranian-born author of the acclaimed graphic novel and film offered a deeply personal account of Iran's Islamic Revolution.

stories.title: Stuck on oil: Can Hawaii power itself?
stories.summary: Hawaii imports much of its fuel — and pays the price. From solar to geothermal, the state is searching for a way out of fossil fuel dependence.

stories.title: Tiananmen dissident lambasts China on massacre anniversary
stories.summary: On the anniversary of the Tiananmen Square massacre, Wu'er Kaixi, who played a leading role during the 1989 protests, spoke to DW about his personal loss and nearly four decades in exile.

stories.title: Mount Everest: Missing Sherpa found alive after 6 days
stories.summary: A Nepalese Sherpa miraculously survived for nearly a week after going missing on May 29. The missing man was found near Everest base camp.

stories.title: Germany's UN defeat: What went wrong?
stories.summary: Austria and Portugal have won seats on the UN Security Council, ending Germany's 40-year streak. DW examines the foreign policy questions raised by Berlin's defeat.

stories.title: Germans at Russia's SPIEF: Who's really there?
stories.summary: Russian officials and media claim German businesses are comming back to the St. Petersburg Economic Forum despite the war in Ukraine. But who is actually attending Russia's flagship event this year?

stories.title: Germany: Lufthansa plane suffers nose gear collapse
stories.summary: Several Lufthansa crew members were injured after the nose landing gear of a parked plane "unexpectedly retracted" at Frankfurt Airport.

stories.title: Iran war: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon-Israel ceasefire terms
stories.summary: The Iran-backed group Hezbollah said it will reject any deal allowing Israel to stay in southern Lebanon, while Israel said it won't withdraw from the area near the border. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: China bans four New Zealand MPs over Taiwan visit
stories.summary: Upon their return from Taiwan last month, the lawmakers were told that China had banned them for a year.

stories.title: Robodogs and helicopters: One Mexican city's approach to World Cup security
stories.summary: In the shadow of cartel violence, BBC’s Will Grant got a first-hand look at security plans in Monterrey.

stories.title: Indian film union drops boycott call against Bollywood star Ranveer Singh
stories.summary: The producers of Don 3 had earlier complained that Singh's alleged exit from the film had caused major financial losses.

stories.title: Cuba’s fuel crisis leaves Havana’s streets overflowing with rubbish
stories.summary: Cuba is facing a mounting waste management crisis as fuel shortages continue to disrupt rubbish collection across Havana, the country's capital.

stories.title: US House delivers rebuke to Trump as it votes to halt Iran war
stories.summary: In the 215-208 vote, four Republicans joined Democrats to pass the measure, which is largely symbolic.

stories.title: France: Protesters rally outside CNews TV over alleged Kremlin influence
stories.summary: France witnessed renewed debate over foreign influence in the media on 3 June as dozens of protesters gathered outside CNews headquarters in Paris to denounce Ksenia Fedorova, the former head of RT France.

stories.title: Trump to nominate Blanche for attorney general on permanent basis
stories.summary: If confirmed by the Senate, Trump's former personal lawyer would become the administration's number one prosecutor.

stories.summary: In an unexpected statement on Thursday, Iran’s Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei said the “malicious enemy” has been defeated with “a decisive blow” to the US, in a possible signal of preparing the Iranian nation for an impending peace deal.

stories.title: Sherpa guide believed dead on Everest found alive after a week
stories.summary: A Sherpa guide missing on Mount Everest for nearly a week was found alive crawling toward base camp and reunited with his family, who had already begun funeral rituals believing he had died.

stories.title: Missing Sherpa guide found on Mount Everest after 'miracle' self-rescue
stories.summary: Cleaners found Dawa Sherpa crawling towards Base Camp six days after he went missing at a higher altitude.

stories.title: Defiance in Beirut's Hezbollah stronghold during fragile ceasefire
stories.summary: BBC correspondent John Sudworth reports from the Beirut stronghold of Hezbollah support, Dahieh.

stories.summary: Women and children were among those killed when Israeli aircraft struck at least four residential buildings in several areas.

stories.summary: Washington wants in on Azerbaijan's pipelines and energy infrastructure, a senior US official said in Baku, as the two countries deepen economic ties following Trump's White House peace deal with Armenia.

stories.title: There is no energy transition — only addition, experts say at Baku Energy Week
stories.summary: Grid infrastructure emerged as the forum's defining bottleneck on the last day of Baku Energy Week, as the IGU's chief of staff said that global consumption of oil, gas, coal and renewables is rising simultaneously, directly challenging the conventional narrative.

stories.title: Sri Lanka care home owner in custody after overnight fire kills 12, officials say
stories.summary: Police said initial witness accounts suggested that the fire originated in a storeroom where dozens of mattresses were stacked.

stories.title: Ukraine strikes St Petersburg oil refinery – video
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones hit energy and military sites in St Petersburg early on Wednesday, according to footage shared by Volodymyr Zelenskyy on X. Several long-range drones crashed into oil storage facilities as Russian air defences tried unsuccessfully to shoot them down. There were loud explosions and black smoke from the blazing oil terminal rose high above the city Ukraine strikes St Petersburg oil refinery Continue reading...

stories.title: Portugal: General strike causes disruption to services
stories.summary: Flights have been canceled and schools closed as unions stage a strike over government labor reform plans. It is the second major walkout in Portugal in six months.

stories.title: Iranian drone attack causes heavy damage to Kuwait airport as US and Iran trade strikes
stories.summary: Kuwait suspended commercial flights after drones heavily damaged its international airport, hours after Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain and the US struck an Iranian military facility on Qeshm Island. Iran's IRGC said it had targeted the US Navy's 5th Fleet headquarters in Bahrain.

stories.title: Bolivian ministers resign as weeks of protests against economic crisis rock government

stories.title: DRC Ebola outbreak could have begun as early as January, WHO chief says
stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus says the virus ‘had a big head start’ but that the response was catching up The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo could have begun as early as January, the head of the World Health Organization said, giving the virus “a big head start”. Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus also said the response was being hindered by blanket travel restrictions and highlighted high levels of community mistrust and low levels of contact tracing as key concerns. Continue re

stories.title: Wim Wenders withdraws 1975 film featuring 13-year-old Nastassja Kinski topless
stories.summary: German director says he recognises actor should have been better protected during filming of Wrong Move German director Wim Wenders has withdrawn from circulation his 1975 film Wrong Move, because of a scene featuring a child actor topless who was 13 at the time of filming. The director said in a statement released on Wednesday: “Streaming, TV and distribution partners have been instructed to no longer make the film publicly accessible.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Japan sees shortage of plastic bags, trays and gloves, as Iran war-induced naphtha shortage worsens
stories.summary: The Middle East is Japan’s main source of crude oil, from which naphtha is extracted and used to make items including printing ink and plastics Takeaways, supermarkets, and bakeries in Japan are running out of plastic bags, trays and food service gloves amid widening shortages of the key plastic ingredient, naphtha, due to the Middle East crisis. The food sector accounts for nearly one-third of Japan’s annual plastic use of more than 8m tonnes, and price rises and shortages are hitting hard acro

stories.title: US House passes war powers resolution to curb Trump’s authority in Iran
stories.summary: Stunning rebuke to president as lawmakers vote 215-208 for measure forcing him to seek congressional approval The US House of Representatives delivered a stunning rebuke to Donald Trump over his war on Iran on Wednesday, as representatives backed a move to force him to seek approval from Congress or withdraw US forces. The House voted 215 to 208 in favor of the war powers resolution, as four Republicans voted with Democrats. The dissident Republicans were Thomas Massie of Kentucky, Brian Fitzpat

stories.title: FBI agents fatally shoot man holding hostages in California building, police say
stories.summary: Nearby buildings, including City Hall and the police headquarters, were evacuated and some roads were temporarily closed during the hostage situation.

stories.title: Kyrgyzstan elected to UN Security Council for first time in its history
stories.summary: Kyrgyzstan was elected to the UN Security Council as a non-permanent member for 2027-2028, marking a rare win for Central Asia.

stories.title: Grab what you can while you can: The new reality in the South China Sea

stories.title: Dohányellenes sikerből maffiapiac? Az ausztrál példa sötét oldala
stories.summary: Ausztrália évtizedeken át a hagyományos dohányzás visszaszorításának egyik kiemelkedő nemzetközi példája volt. Elsőként vezetett be egységes, márkajelzés nélküli cigarettásdobozokat, eltüntette a dohányreklámokat, erős képi figyelmeztetéseket tett a csomagolásra, kitiltotta a dohányzást a legtöbb nyilvános térből, és folyamatosan emelte a legális dohánytermékek jövedéki adóját. A cél világos volt: legyen drága, kényelmetlen, társadalmilag egyre kevésbé elfogadott szokás.

stories.summary: With corrupt police on the streets and shopkeepers forced to pay gangs, president has vowed to tackle crime that now affects all parts of society It was about 11pm and Luis* was about to get into an Uber to go home when the police car pulled up. One of the officers frisked him and produced two plastic bags with what looked like drugs: one contained some sort of powder, the other little crystals. Luis had never seen them before. stories.title: UK: Protest erupts over student murder
stories.summary: Protesters have clashed with police in Southampton over the December murder of finance student Henry Nowak. The fatally injured victim was handcuffed by police after his killer accused him of racist abuse.

stories.title: EU Commission set to slam Italy's fuel duty cuts as energy prices spike
stories.summary: The EU Commission is set to criticise the Italian government's reduction in excise duties on fuels in a report to be published on Wednesday. Rome has been asking for more fiscal flexibility to address the energy crisis, which Brussels deems should be directed at vulnerable families and industries.

stories.title: ‘You’re f****** crazy’: Trump clashes with Netanyahu as Lebanon tensions rise
stories.summary: Trump rebukes Benjamin Netanyahu over Lebanon escalation, warning strikes risk isolating Israel and derailing Iran talks, according to reports

stories.title: US and Iran exchange new strikes as ceasefire talks falter over Israel's military actions in Lebanon
stories.summary: Iran fired missiles toward Kuwait and Bahrain but failed to hit their targets, with two of those fired at Kuwait falling apart en route, while those aimed at Bahrain were intercepted, the US Central Command said.

stories.title: UK: Police and protesters clash at Southampton rally over student killing
stories.summary: Clashes broke out between protesters and police in Southampton after the release of bodycam footage linked to the fatal stabbing of student Henry Nowak sparked public anger.

stories.title: 11 officers injured in Henry Nowak murder protests in the UK, police say
stories.summary: In a joint statement, a conglomerate of Sikh community groups condemned what it called a "moment of madness by an individual".

stories.title: Drone attack on UAE's Barakah more dangerous than Zaporizhzhia, IAEA chief tells Euronews
stories.summary: Rafael Grossi told Euronews the drone strike on the operating UAE nuclear plant posed greater risks than Zaporizhzhia because its reactors were live at the time of the attack, warning that tonnes of nuclear material could have triggered a radiological accident with "very, very serious consequences".

stories.title: Ukrainian drones hit St Petersburg as Putin's flagship economic forum opens
stories.summary: Several long-range drones struck oil storage facilities near the city, days before Vladimir Putin is due to address the event.

stories.title: At least 21 people, mostly foreign nationals, killed in New Delhi hotel fire, police say
stories.summary: Several of those killed were foreigners, mainly from Central Asia and Africa, the Press Trust of India news agency reported, quoting unnamed officials.

stories.title: Hundreds of Russian strikes across Ukraine kill at least 14 people – video
stories.summary: Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukraine overnight, according to the Ukrainian air force, including eight hypersonic Tsirkon missiles. The main targets were Kyiv, Dnipro, Zaporizhzhia, Poltava and Kharkiv. The Ukrainian president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has asked his US counterpart, Donald Trump, to send Patriot missiles to the country after the Russian attacks killed at least 14 people and injured dozens more Zelenskyy asks Trump to send missiles after Russian strikes across Ukraine

stories.title: UK wins court case over canceled plan to deport asylum seekers to Rwanda
stories.summary: An international court has rejected a Rwandan claim for millions in damages over a scrapped deal to send asylum seekers to the East African nation.

stories.title: Republicans press Trump over controversial €1.5bn fund for victims of 'political weaponisation'
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump's Anti-Weaponisation Fund, launched to compensate individuals who believe they were subjected to politically motivated investigations, is being questioned by senators in his own party.

stories.title: ‘We don’t have another country to run to’: Kenyans fear US plan for Ebola quarantine site
stories.summary: People from town of potential site for US citizens exposed to Ebola say it puts them at risk in country with no known cases People from a town in central Kenya where the US wants to set up an Ebola quarantine facility for its citizens have strongly criticised the plan, saying they fear it will expose them to the virus and that it is indicative of double standards on the part of the US. “Everybody should be quarantined in their home country. We shouldn’t allow foreigners to bring us diseases,” sa

stories.title: Germany: Cologne Cathedral to charge tourists €12 for entry
stories.summary: Cologne Cathedral has laid out plans to charge tourist visitors, starting in July. They will be asked to pay €12 each, to help cover maintenance costs. It still plans to keep a small free area available for worshippers.

stories.title: Armenia's election offers a choice between Russia and EU
stories.summary: Armenians are set to vote on the country's future geopolitical direction on June 7. The election will be a test of public opinion in the long-standing Russia ally, where the population is increasingly eyeing the EU.

stories.title: Who owns the billions in Germany's dormant bank accounts?
stories.summary: Billions of euros sit untouched in dormant bank accounts in Germany. With heirs unaware and no central register in place, banks and politicians are debating who should ultimately control the money.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia and Russia draw closer amid OPEC upheaval
stories.summary: As Saudi Arabia and Russia deepen cooperation, their combined influence over global oil supply is growing. Could this shift the balance of power in energy markets?

stories.title: Google mosquito army: Scientists say 'we must take action'
stories.summary: Google's Debug research program plans to release millions of sterile mosquitoes to fight species that spread diseases like dengue. How does the method work — and should humans interfere with nature like this?

stories.title: Wim Wenders withdraws film with disputed child nude scene
stories.summary: Actress Nastassja Kinski spent years calling for the removal of a sexualized scene from Wim Wenders' film "The Wrong Move," in which she appeared topless at the age of 13.

stories.title: Southern energy corridors set to dominate supplies to Europe, global leaders say
stories.summary: The rapid reshape of global energy supply routes and markets is a key focus at the Baku Energy Week 2026.

stories.title: IRGC says Iran has more 'trump cards' up its sleeve as talks hang by a thread
stories.summary: A senior IRGC general said Iran had not revealed all its military capabilities and was ready for direct confrontation with the US and NATO, as hardliners linked to acting IRGC commander Ahmad Vahidi were identified as the force behind Monday's suspension of indirect negotiations with Washington.

stories.title: Massive drug tunnel uncovered beneath US-Mexico border
stories.summary: US authorities released footage of a sophisticated tunnel running beneath the US-Mexico border, saying it was used to smuggle more than $45 million worth of cocaine from Tijuana into California.

stories.title: French artist JR's giant 'cave' installation in Paris appears damaged
stories.summary: Strong winds and heavy rain damaged artist JR’s giant “La Caverne” installation covering Paris’ Pont Neuf bridge, forcing organisers to postpone its public opening scheduled for June 6.

stories.title: A man in Turkey became one of the richest people in the world without realising it
stories.summary: With a balance of around $21.8 billion in his account, Takalo ranks among the world's super rich, but he has been unable to access his money for nearly a month.

stories.title: Foreign nationals among at least 21 killed in Delhi fire
stories.summary: Many victims were South Asians who had travelled to India for treatment or to accompany relatives.

stories.title: Fire tears through New Delhi building, killing at least 18
stories.summary: A fire in a New Delhi building killed at least 18 people and injured several others, with rescue teams pulling dozens from the Malviya Nagar block.

stories.title: Trump confirms calling Netanyahu 'f****** crazy' in telephone row over Lebanon strikes
stories.summary: The comments provide a rare public glimpse into tensions between the two leaders, who have long presented a united front despite occasional disagreements behind closed doors.

stories.title: SOCAR, TotalEnergies and ADNOC lock in Caspian gas supply deal
stories.summary: Azerbaijan's SOCAR signed a long-term deal with TotalEnergies, ADNOC's investment arm XRG and Turkey's BOTAŞ to export gas from the Absheron offshore field from 2029, bolstering the Southern Gas Corridor that Europe has increasingly relied on in recent years.

stories.title: Refugees and migration: Is Europe closing its doors?
stories.summary: Migration researchers have called on European governments to abandon isolationist policies. But many politicians are not heeding their calls.

stories.title: Two people shot dead in Kenya protests against Ebola plan
stories.summary: Kenyan police shot and killed two people who were protesting a US-backed plan to establish an Ebola quarantine facility in central Kenya.

stories.title: Italy marks 80 years of the Republic with military parade in Rome
stories.summary: Italy celebrated the 80th anniversary of the birth of the Republic with ceremonies, military parades and flypasts in Rome, commemorating the 1946 vote that paved the way for democracy.

stories.title: Explosion at Malta fireworks factory sends plumes of smoke into air – video
stories.summary: The explosion happened at the Ta' Lourdes factory on the north side of the Mediterranean island on Monday morning. Two men in fields near the factory at the time of the blast were admitted to hospital with minor injuries. Residents in the surrounding area reported shattered windows and buildings shaken by the force of the blast. It is not yet known what caused the explosion Continue reading...

stories.title: Ethiopia goes electric following gas car ban
stories.summary: Electric vehicles have taken off in Ethiopia. Key to the shift: a world-first ban on importing fossil fuel-powered vehicles.

stories.title: Legendary warrior women: The history of the Amazons
stories.summary: They're said to have cut off their breasts to better draw their bows and even to have killed their male offspring. What is the history behind the myths surrounding the Amazons?

stories.title: Unpacking Germany's campaign for a UN Security Council seat
stories.summary: On Wednesday, the United Nations will hold elections for several seats on the UN Security Council. Germany's government is campaigning vigorously for a seat.

stories.title: Kosovo general election: the cost of political polarization
stories.summary: Sunday's election in Kosovo — the third in under 16 months — is marked by a clash between former allies PM Albin Kurti and ex-President Vjosa Osmani, deadlock in the dialogue with Serbia and obstacles to EU integration.

stories.title: Watch: Explosion at fireworks factory in Malta
stories.summary: Two men were injured in the explosion and were taken to hospital with minor injuries.

stories.title: Cryptocurrency addiction is real — and it looks just like gambling
stories.summary: Jamie Giles is a therapist at Castle Craig, one of Scotland's leading addiction treatment centres. He spoke to Euronews about how to spot problematic crypto investing, who is most at risk and what to do about it.

stories.title: China's highest bridge attracts millions of visitors
stories.summary: China's Huajiang Grand Canyon Bridge has cut travel times, expanded 5G access and helped boost tourism and business in rural Guizhou.

stories.title: Half of Belgium sees the US as a bigger adversary than China
stories.summary: According to a recent poll, over 50 percent of Belgians view the US as an adversary. Meanwhile, only 40% to 44% perceive China as a rival.

stories.title: New Aukus drone tech to protect critical undersea cables as Marles warns: ‘seabed is a battlefield’
stories.summary: Minister at Singapore defence summit also reveals Australia to buy only secondhand Aukus submarines from US Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The defence minister, Richard Marles, has said the “seabed is a battlefield” in a combative speech urging Beijing to be more transparent about its maritime operations, and taking aim at weak international controls over so-called “shadow-fleet” vessels. The warning came as the
